student_outcomes.text: Analyze a complex computing problem and apply principles of computing and other relevant disciplines to identify solutions
Design, implement, and evaluate a computing-based solution to meet a given set of computing requirements in the context of the program's discipline
Communicate effectively in a variety of professional contexts
Recognize professional responsibilities and make informed judgments in computing practice based on legal and ethical principles
Function effectively as a member or leader of a team engaged in activities appropriate to the program's discipline
Apply computer science theory and software development fundamentals to produce computing-based solutions

curriculum_requirements.design_experience: SO-2 requires a design experience integrating multiple knowledge areas
curriculum_requirements.notes: Operating systems is explicitly named as a required topic area. Each SO must be assessed in at least 2 courses with documented evidence.

assessment_requirements.documentation: Each course must map assessments to SOs with documentation
assessment_requirements.annual_report: Annual assessment report submitted to department committee
assessment_requirements.review_process: ABET evaluators review syllabi, student work samples, and assessment data during visits (every 6 years)

constraints_for_agent: MUST NOT recommend changes that eliminate coverage of any SO currently mapped to a course without confirming another course covers that SO
MUST flag when a proposed change reduces direct assessment points for any SO below the minimum of 2 across the program
Changes to assessment instruments require updating the department assessment plan, which needs committee approval
Operating systems is a REQUIRED topic area — cannot be replaced or substantially reduced
